replication_study.hypothesis: There is an interaction between participant culture (European-/European-heritage vs. East-Asian heritage) and writing condition (mortality-salience prime vs. control dental-pain prime) on Prostitution Attitude Scale (PAS) scores: after mortality salience, East-Asian-heritage participants will report more positive attitudes toward prostitution whereas European-heritage participants will report more negative attitudes, relative to their respective control conditions.

data_plan.source_type: Online experimental survey (variables include Prolific flag, writing task, PAS items, PANAS, bail amount, demographics).
data_plan.sample_size: Will be read directly from the dataset at run-time; expected N≈150-250 based on online replication recruitment (variable ‘Prolific’ present).
data_plan.access_details: The .sav file is shipped in /app/data and is freely available for scientific replication.

qualification.explanation: The dataset mirrors the original experimental design (same manipulation, measures, and analysis strategy) but was collected independently from the original UCSB lab sample, thereby providing an ideal independent test of robustness.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Contains identical operationalisations: (a) mortality-salience vs. dental-pain writing prime (variable WritingCondition); (b) Prostitution Attitude Scale items PAS1–PAS5 with reverse–coded items exactly matching Rosenblatt et al. (1989) and used in the original Ma-Kellams & Blascovich (2011) study (see original_paper.pdf, pp. 1020–1021); (c) ethnicity coding variables that allow construction of the European- vs. Asian-American categories required for the 2×2 ANOVA (see SPSS syntax lines COMPUTE EuropeanAmerican / EastAsian).
qualification.deviation_from_original: Participants were recruited online (variable Prolific) instead of in-person undergraduate lab (post_registration.json, data.source field). The sample likely includes international East-Asian–born respondents (EastAsianBorn variable) and possibly larger age variance. These differences provide a new context/sample while preserving the manipulation and measures.

data_plan.notes: .sav binary format must be read with pyreadstat. Variable labels are absent; all IO paths are routed through /app/data. BailAmount is highly skewed (see SPSS syntax for log transform) but is secondary; focal replication uses PASAverage.

steps: Construct variables exactly as in the provided SPSS syntax: reverse code PAS2 and PAS5, take mean to obtain PASAverage; code Culture (0=European American, 1=East Asian); retain WritingCondition (0=control, 1=mortality).
Exclude cases with missing values on Culture, WritingCondition, or PASAverage (listwise).
Extract the Culture×WritingCondition interaction F-statistic, p-value, and partial-η².
Optionally replicate planned simple-effects contrasts for each culture separately.
Write cleaned data and analysis results to /app/data.
planned_method.models: Two-way fixed-effects ANOVA (ordinary least squares) with Type-III sums of squares.
planned_method.outcome_variable: PASAverage – mean of 5 PAS items (after reverse-coding).
planned_method.independent_variables: Culture (European American = 0; East Asian = 1) and WritingCondition (control = 0; mortality = 1).
planned_method.control_variables: Mood (PANASNetScore) will be inspected; main replication omits it but we will report whether adding it changes conclusions.
planned_method.tools_software: Python 3.10; pandas, numpy, pyreadstat, statsmodels, scipy (installed via pip).

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Partial η² and F statistic for the Culture × WritingCondition interaction term in the ANOVA model.

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion for rows with missing Culture, WritingCondition, or PASAverage.
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Not required (single focal interaction); any exploratory contrasts will be labeled exploratory and uncorrected.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Replication considered successful if (a) the interaction F-test is in the same directional pattern (simple effects replicate) AND (b) p < 0.05 two-tailed; effect-size similarity (partial η² within ±0.05) will also be reported.

files.analyze_replication.py: Main entry point. Loads SPSS data, constructs variables, performs 2×2 ANOVA using statsmodels, prints and saves results, and writes a cleaned CSV copy to /app/data/ma_kellams_replication_cleaned.csv.

codebase.notes: The script is a Python translation of the provided SPSS syntax (Ma-Kellams Replication Analysis Syntax.sps). All paths are hard-coded to /app/data to obey container IO policy.

analysis.instructions: Inside the container, run:  python /app/data/analyze_replication.py.  The script prints the Type-III ANOVA table to stdout and saves cleaned data for further inspection.
analysis.comparison_metrics: Direction and significance (p-value) of the Culture×WritingCondition interaction; partial-η² effect size; comparison of group means pattern with original (European Americans lower PAS under mortality; East Asians higher).
